12: 
13: // A CredentialStore contains credential libraries. It is owned by a project.
14: type CredentialStore struct {
15: 	*store.CredentialStore
16: 	tableName string `gorm:"-"`
17: 
18: 	clientCert  *ClientCertificate `gorm:"-"`
19: 	inputToken  TokenSecret        `gorm:"-"`
20: 	outputToken *Token             `gorm:"-"`
21: 
22: 	privateClientCert *ClientCertificate `gorm:"-"`
23: 	privateToken      *Token             `gorm:"-"`
24: }
25: 
26: // NewCredentialStore creates a new in memory CredentialStore for a Vault
27: // server at vaultAddress assigned to projectId. Name, description, CA cert,
28: // client cert, namespace, TLS server name, worker filter, and TLS skip verify are the
29: // only valid options. All other options are ignored.
30: func NewCredentialStore(projectId string, vaultAddress string, token TokenSecret, opt ...Option) (*CredentialStore, error) {
31: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
32: 	cs := &CredentialStore{
33: 		inputToken: token,
34: 		clientCert: opts.withClientCert,
35: 		CredentialStore: &store.CredentialStore{
36: 			ProjectId:     projectId,
37: 			Name:          opts.withName,
38: 			Description:   opts.withDescription,
39: 			VaultAddress:  vaultAddress,
40: 			CaCert:        opts.withCACert,
41: 			Namespace:     opts.withNamespace,
42: 			TlsServerName: opts.withTlsServerName,
43: 			TlsSkipVerify: opts.withTlsSkipVerify,
44: 			WorkerFilter:  opts.withWorkerFilter,
45: 		},
46: 	}
47: 	return cs, nil
48: }
49: 
50: func allocCredentialStore() *CredentialStore {
51: 	return &CredentialStore{
52: 		CredentialStore: &store.CredentialStore{},
53: 	}
54: }
55: 
56: func (cs *CredentialStore) clone() *CredentialStore {
57: 	tokenCopy := make(TokenSecret, len(cs.inputToken))
58: 	copy(tokenCopy, cs.inputToken)
59: 	var clientCertCopy *ClientCertificate
60: 	if cs.clientCert != nil {
61: 		clientCertCopy = cs.clientCert.clone()
62: 	}
63: 	cp := proto.Clone(cs.CredentialStore)
64: 	return &CredentialStore{
65: 		inputToken:      tokenCopy,
66: 		clientCert:      clientCertCopy,
67: 		CredentialStore: cp.(*store.CredentialStore),
68: 	}
69: }
70: 
71: // applyUpdate returns a new CredentialStore with the new values applied to
72: // this based on the passed in fieldMaskPaths.
73: func (cs *CredentialStore) applyUpdate(new *CredentialStore, fieldMaskPaths []string) *CredentialStore {
74: 	cp := cs.clone()
75: 	for _, f := range fieldMaskPaths {
76: 		switch {
77: 		case strings.EqualFold(nameField, f):
78: 			cp.Name = new.Name
79: 		case strings.EqualFold(descriptionField, f):
80: 			cp.Description = new.Description
81: 		case strings.EqualFold(certificateField, f):
82: 			if new.clientCert == nil {
83: 				cp.clientCert = nil
84: 				continue
85: 			}
86: 			if cp.clientCert == nil {
87: 				cp.clientCert = allocClientCertificate()
88: 			}
89: 			cp.clientCert.Certificate = new.clientCert.GetCertificate()
90: 			cp.clientCert.StoreId = cs.GetPublicId()
91: 		case strings.EqualFold(certificateKeyField, f):
92: 			if new.clientCert == nil {
93: 				cp.clientCert = nil
94: 				continue
95: 			}
96: 			if cp.clientCert == nil {
97: 				cp.clientCert = allocClientCertificate()
98: 			}
99: 			cp.clientCert.CertificateKey = new.clientCert.GetCertificateKey()
100: 			cp.clientCert.StoreId = cs.GetPublicId()
101: 		case strings.EqualFold(vaultAddressField, f):
102: 			cp.VaultAddress = new.VaultAddress
103: 		case strings.EqualFold(namespaceField, f):
104: 			cp.Namespace = new.Namespace
105: 		case strings.EqualFold(caCertField, f):
106: 			cp.CaCert = new.CaCert
107: 		case strings.EqualFold(tlsServerNameField, f):
108: 			cp.TlsServerName = new.TlsServerName
109: 		case strings.EqualFold(tlsSkipVerifyField, f):
110: 			cp.TlsSkipVerify = new.TlsSkipVerify
111: 		case strings.EqualFold(tokenField, f):
112: 			cp.inputToken = new.inputToken
113: 		case strings.EqualFold(workerFilterField, f):
114: 			cp.WorkerFilter = new.WorkerFilter
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: 	return cp
118: }
119: 
120: // TableName returns the table name.
121: func (cs *CredentialStore) TableName() string {
122: 	if cs.tableName != "" {
123: 		return cs.tableName
124: 	}
125: 	return "credential_vault_store"
126: }
127: 
128: // SetTableName sets the table name.
129: func (cs *CredentialStore) SetTableName(n string) {
130: 	cs.tableName = n
131: }

133: func (cs *CredentialStore) oplog(op oplog.OpType) oplog.Metadata {
134: 	metadata := oplog.Metadata{
135: 		"resource-public-id": []string{cs.PublicId},
136: 		"resource-type":      []string{"credential-vault-store"},
137: 		"op-type":            []string{op.String()},
138: 	}
139: 	if cs.ProjectId != "" {
140: 		metadata["project-id"] = []string{cs.ProjectId}
141: 	}
142: 	return metadata
143: }

146: 	msg := oplog.Message{
147: 		Message:  cs.clone(),
148: 		TypeName: cs.TableName(),
149: 	}
150: 	switch opType {

152: 		msg.OpType = oplog.OpType_OP_TYPE_CREATE
153: 	case db.UpdateOp:

155: 	case db.DeleteOp:

157: 	}
158: 	return &msg

164: }

354: 	var inClauseSpots []string
355: 	for i := 1; i < len(libIds)+1; i++ {
356: 		inClauseSpots = append(inClauseSpots, fmt.Sprintf("@%d", i))
357: 	}
358: 	inClause := strings.Join(inClauseSpots, ",")
359: 
360: 	query := fmt.Sprintf(selectPrivateLibrariesQuery, inClause)
361: 
362: 	var params []interface{}
363: 	for idx, v := range libIds {
364: 		params = append(params, sql.Named(fmt.Sprintf("%d", idx+1), v))
365: 	}
366: 	rows, err := r.reader.Query(ctx, query, params)
367: 	if err != nil {
368: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("query failed"))
369: 	}
370: 	defer rows.Close()
371:

373: 	for rows.Next() {
374: 		var lib privateLibrary
375: 		if err := r.reader.ScanRows(ctx, rows, &lib); err != nil {
376: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("scan row failed"))
377: 		}
378: 		purps := mapper.get(lib.GetPublicId())
379: 		if len(purps) == 0 {
380: 			return nil, errors.E(ctx, errors.WithCode(errors.InvalidParameter), errors.WithMsg("unknown library"))
381: 		}
382: 		for _, purp := range purps {

385: 			libs = append(libs, cp)

387: 	}

391: 		if err != nil {

393: 		}

395: 		if err := pl.decrypt(ctx, databaseWrapper); err != nil {

403: // requestMap takes a slice of credential requests and provides of list of
404: // library IDs with duplicate IDs removed. It also provides a way to lookup

408: // each credential is for a different purpose. When retrieving the private
409: // libraries from the database, a list of library IDs with duplicates
410: // removed is needed. When requesting credentials from vault, any library
411: // being used for multiple purposes needs to be duplicated with the purpose
412: // so multiple requests are made to vault using the same library.

421: 			for _, purp := range purps {
422: 				if purp == req.Purpose {
423: 					return nil, errors.EDeprecated(errors.WithCode(errors.InvalidParameter), errors.WithMsg("duplicate library and purpose"))
424: 				}
425: 			}
426: 		}
427: 		ids[req.SourceId] = append(ids[req.SourceId], req.Purpose)

431: 	}, nil

349: // Run queries the vault credential repo for tokens that need to be revoked, it then creates
350: // a vault client and revokes each token.  Can not be run in parallel, if Run is invoked while
351: // already running an error with code JobAlreadyRunning will be returned.
352: func (r *TokenRevocationJob) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
353: 	const op = "vault.(TokenRevocationJob).Run"
354: 	if !r.running.CAS(r.running.Load(), true) {
355: 		return errors.New(ctx, errors.JobAlreadyRunning, op, "job already running")
356: 	}
357: 	defer r.running.Store(false)
358: 
359: 	// Verify context is not done before running
360: 	if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
361: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
362: 	}
363: 
364: 	// Fetch all tokens in the revoke state as well as all tokens in the maintaining state
365: 	// that have no credentials in an active state.
366: 	where := `
367: token_status = 'revoke'
368: or
369: (token_status = 'maintaining'
370:   and token_hmac not in (
371:     select token_hmac from credential_vault_credential 
372:      where status = 'active'
373: ))
374: `
375: 
376: 	var ps []*privateStore
377: 	err := r.reader.SearchWhere(ctx, &ps, where, nil, db.WithLimit(r.limit))
378: 	if err != nil {
379: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	// Set numProcessed and numTokens for s report
383: 	r.numProcessed, r.numTokens = 0, len(ps)
384: 	for _, s := range ps {
385: 		// Verify context is not done before renewing next token
386: 		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
387: 			return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
388: 		}
389: 		if err := r.revokeToken(ctx, s); err != nil {
390: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error revoking token", "credential store id", s.StoreId))
391: 		}
392: 		r.numProcessed++
393: 	}
394: 
395: 	return nil
396: }

398: func (r *TokenRevocationJob) revokeToken(ctx context.Context, s *privateStore) error {
399: 	const op = "vault.(TokenRevocationJob).revokeToken"
400: 	databaseWrapper, err := r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, s.ProjectId, kms.KeyPurposeDatabase)
401: 	if err != nil {
402: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get database wrapper"))
403: 	}
404: 	if err = s.decrypt(ctx, databaseWrapper); err != nil {
405: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
406: 	}
407: 
408: 	token := s.token()
409: 	if token == nil {
410: 		// Store has no token to revoke
411: 		return nil
412: 	}
413: 
414: 	vc, err := s.client()
415: 	if err != nil {
416: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
417: 	}
418: 
419: 	var respErr *vault.ResponseError
420: 	err = vc.revokeToken()
421: 	if ok := errors.As(err, &respErr); ok && respErr.StatusCode == http.StatusForbidden {
422: 		// Vault returned a 403 when attempting a revoke self, the token is already expired.
423: 		// Clobber error and set status to "revoked" below.
424: 		err = nil
425: 	}
426: 	if err != nil {
427: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to revoke vault token"))
428: 	}
429: 
430: 	query, values := token.updateStatusQuery(RevokedToken)
431: 	numRows, err := r.writer.Exec(ctx, query, values)
432: 	if err != nil {
433: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
434: 	}
435: 	if numRows != 1 {
436: 		return errors.New(ctx, errors.Unknown, op, "token revoked but failed to update repo")
437: 	}
438: 
439: 	// Set credentials associated with this token to revoked as Vault will already cascade revoke them
440: 	_, err = r.writer.Exec(ctx, updateCredentialStatusByTokenQuery, []interface{}{RevokedCredential, token.TokenHmac})
441: 	if err != nil {
442: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error updating credentials to revoked after revoking token"))
443: 	}
444: 
445: 	return nil
446: }

520: 	}

525: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
526: 	}

529: 	// Fetch all active credentials that will reach their renewal point within the renewalWindow.
530: 	// This is done to avoid constantly scheduling the credential renewal job when there are
531: 	// multiple credentials set to renew in sequence.

540: 		// Verify context is not done before renewing next token

543: 		}

554: func (r *CredentialRenewalJob) renewCred(ctx context.Context, c *privateCredential) error {
555: 	const op = "vault.(CredentialRenewalJob).renewCred"
556: 	databaseWrapper, err := r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, c.ProjectId, kms.KeyPurposeDatabase)
557: 	if err != nil {
558: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get database wrapper"))
559: 	}
560: 	if err = c.decrypt(ctx, databaseWrapper); err != nil {
561: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
562: 	}
563: 
564: 	vc, err := c.client()
565: 	if err != nil {
566: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
567: 	}
568: 	cred := c.toCredential()
569: 
570: 	var respErr *vault.ResponseError
571: 	// Subtract last renewal time from previous expiration time to get lease duration
572: 	leaseDuration := c.ExpirationTime.AsTime().Sub(c.LastRenewalTime.AsTime())
573: 	renewedCred, err := vc.renewLease(c.ExternalId, leaseDuration)
574: 	if ok := errors.As(err, &respErr); ok && respErr.StatusCode == http.StatusBadRequest {
575: 		// Vault returned a 400 when attempting a renew lease, the lease is either expired
576: 		// or the leaseId is malformed.  Set status to "expired".
577: 		query, values := cred.updateStatusQuery(ExpiredCredential)
578: 		numRows, err := r.writer.Exec(ctx, query, values)
579: 		if err != nil {
580: 			return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
581: 		}
582: 		if numRows != 1 {
583: 			return errors.New(ctx, errors.Unknown, op, "credential expired but failed to update repo")
584: 		}
585: 		return nil
586: 	}
587: 	if err != nil {
588: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to renew credential"))
589: 	}
590: 
591: 	cred.expiration = time.Duration(renewedCred.LeaseDuration) * time.Second
592: 	query, values := cred.updateExpirationQuery()
593: 	numRows, err := r.writer.Exec(ctx, query, values)
594: 	if err != nil {
595: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
596: 	}
597: 	if numRows != 1 {
598: 		return errors.New(ctx, errors.Unknown, op, "credential renewed but failed to update repo")
599: 	}
600: 
601: 	return nil
602: }
